primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer si des clauses contractuelles pouvant modifier le calendrier ou le montant des flux de trésorerie, y compris des clauses conditionnelles avec événement déclencheur, restent compatibles avec le critère SPPI d’un « prêt basique » au sens d’IFRS 9, et donc avec les catégories de classement ultérieur correspondantes.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule by selecting IFRS 9 as the single governing lineage because it directly governs SPPI-based classification of financial assets, and no other higher- or equal-precedence document in the context governs that same issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — the question is squarely about the SPPI test and resulting IFRS 9 classification framework for financial assets.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether contractual cash flow features, including contingent changes in timing or amount, are compatible with SPPI and therefore with the IFRS 9 measurement categories.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify the basic lending arrangement concept and constrain the SPPI analysis for modified time value, leverage, non-recourse features and contractually linked instruments.

treatment_families.family: IFRS 9 classification outcomes available when the instrument's contractual cash flows satisfy SPPI and the business model is to hold to collect
IFRS 9 classification outcomes available when the instrument's contractual cash flows satisfy SPPI and the business model is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling
Residual IFRS 9 classification outcome when contractual cash flows fail SPPI because the contingent or other contractual features are inconsistent with a basic lending arrangement

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies amortised cost as a top-level measurement category when the asset meets SPPI and is held in a hold-to-collect business model.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through other comprehensive income as a top-level measurement category when the asset meets SPPI and is held in a collect-and-sell business model.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through profit or loss as the residual top-level measurement category when the asset does not qualify for amortised cost or fair value through other comprehensive income, including where contingent features are inconsistent with SPPI.
